entities.name: PADDY
entities.description: Lowland rice grown under flooded or submerged conditions

entities.name: FLOODED CONDITION
entities.description: State of soil being saturated with water

entities.name: PUDDLING
entities.description: Traditional practice to reduce water loss in lowland rice fields

entities.name: PLOW SOLE
entities.description: A hydraulically less-conductive layer formed below the root zone due to puddling

entities.name: SOIL CRACKING
entities.description: Formation of cracks in the soil that can lead to preferential flow

entities.name: PREFERENTIAL FLOW
entities.description: Water movement through macropores or cracks, bypassing the soil matrix

entities.name: SOIL WATER REGIME
entities.description: The pattern and distribution of water in the soil

entities.name: IN SITU MEASUREMENT
entities.description: Method for measuring soil hydraulic properties directly in the field

entities.name: SOIL HYDRAULIC PROPERTIES
entities.description: Characteristics of soil that determine the movement of water

entities.name: RAINY SEASON
entities.description: Period of high precipitation from June to October

entities.name: PIEZOMETERS
entities.description: Instruments used to measure piezometric pressure heads in soil layers

entities.name: DRYING AND WETTING CYCLES
entities.description: Periodic changes in soil moisture content

entities.name: MONSOON CLIMATE
entities.description: Climate characterized by seasonal rainfall

entities.name: EASTERN INDIA
entities.description: The geographic region where the field experiment was conducted

entities.name: MARQUARDT-LEVENBERG ALGORITHM
entities.description: An algorithm used for inverse estimation of parameters

entities.name: HYDRUS-1D
entities.description: A simulation environment used to estimate soil hydraulic parameters

entities.name: HYDRAULIC CONDUCTIVITY
entities.description: The ease with which water can move through soil pore spaces

entities.name: EARTHWORM
entities.description: Soil organisms whose burrows contribute to preferential flow

entities.name: SINGLE-POROSITY MODEL
entities.description: A simulation model that treats soil as a single medium for water flow

entities.name: DUAL-POROSITY MODEL
entities.description: A simulation model that distinguishes between matrix flow and preferential flow

entities.name: WATER FLOW
entities.description: The movement of water through the soil profile

entities.name: MACROPORES
entities.description: Large pores in the soil that facilitate preferential transport

entities.name: SOIL MATRIX
entities.description: The bulk of the soil pores through which water moves slowly
